headline: Paris Mayor Grégoire on the climate crisis: 'To adapt together, we need to change our way of life'
subtitle: In an interview with Le Monde, the Socialist leader of the French capital city called individual air conditioning 'a scourge' and urged 'flexibility' and 'makeshift solutions' to respond to the urgent challenge of heat waves.

headline: France records its hottest day ever, with relief on the horizon

Ukrainian strikes on Russian refineries are triggering shortages and rising discontent
subtitle: For the past three months, Kyiv's drones have targeted Russian refineries, ports, depots and pipelines, causing ripple effects throughout the Russian economy. Gasoline has become scarce at service stations.

headline: Two killed in Israeli strike in South Lebanon, Hezbollah denounces ceasefire 'violation'
subtitle: Two people were killed in southern Lebanon on Wednesday in an Israeli drone strike that Hezbollah said was the latest breach of a ceasefire agreement between the two sides.

headline: China remains on the sidelines of the World Cup
subtitle: The global superpower, which relishes the opportunity to excel at major international events, has only managed to qualify once, in 2002. Despite Xi Jinping's determination, the poor performance of the national football team continues to disappoint Chinese fans.

headline: Masculinism, an increasingly widespread, violent and popular ideology among French youth
subtitle: According to a report written by the Sénat's delegation for women's rights and published on Wednesday, the movement, which has close ties to the far right, is now considered an emerging terrorist threat and a cause for serious concern.

subtitle: As France faces extreme heat, many of these top-story spaces have become uninhabitable. Some residents have formed groups to call on their landlords, neighbors and local authorities to address their living conditions.

headline: Montréal gunman leaves behind manifesto inspired by the 'incel' movement
subtitle: A shooting in the Canadian city on Monday, June 22, left three dead, including a police officer and the attacker. In a manifesto, the shooter expressed his hatred of women.

headline: France's public pools need urgent renovation: 'What would we do without them? We wouldn't make it'
subtitle: Aging, energy-intensive and expensive for rural towns to maintain, many aquatic centers in the countryside have been shutting down. Yet these facilities provide equal access for people to cool off and learn to swim. An MP recently sounded the alarm about the worrying situation facing these essential local public services.

headline: Heeding Resistance fighter Marc Bloch's warning against 'strange defeats'
